results.resolved_title: Secretion of the Serratia marcescens HasA protein by an ABC transporter
results.note: Paper is about HasA in Serratia marcescens (secreted by an ABC/T1SS transporter); makes no claim about Halomonas elongata. Cited as source for a homolog in an unrelated species. PMID 8071214.

results.resolved_title: Secretion of the Serratia marcescens HasA protein by an ABC transporter
results.note: Serratia marcescens HasA paper; no claim about Paracidovorax wautersii. Homolog-transfer provenance defect. PMID 8071214.

results.resolved_title: Secretion of the Serratia marcescens HasA protein by an ABC transporter
results.note: Serratia marcescens HasA paper; no claim about Pseudomonas chlororaphis. Homolog-transfer provenance defect. PMID 8071214.

results.resolved_title: Secretion of the Serratia marcescens HasA protein by an ABC transporter
results.note: Serratia marcescens HasA paper; no claim about Pseudomonas extremaustralis. Homolog-transfer provenance defect. PMID 8071214.

results.resolved_title: Secretion of the Serratia marcescens HasA protein by an ABC transporter
results.note: Serratia marcescens HasA paper; no claim about Pseudomonas fluorescens. Homolog-transfer provenance defect. PMID 8071214.

results.resolved_title: Secretion of the Serratia marcescens HasA protein by an ABC transporter
results.note: Serratia marcescens HasA paper; no claim about Pseudomonas lundensis. Homolog-transfer provenance defect. PMID 8071214.

results.note: Serratia marcescens HasA paper; no claim about Yersinia aldovae. Homolog-transfer provenance defect. PMID 8071214.

results.resolved_title: Secretion of the Serratia marcescens HasA protein by an ABC transporter
results.note: Serratia marcescens HasA paper; no claim about Yersinia enterocolitica. Homolog-transfer provenance defect. PMID 8071214.

results.note: Serratia marcescens HasA paper; no claim about Yersinia pestis. Homolog-transfer provenance defect. PMID 8071214.

results.resolved_title: Secretion of the Serratia marcescens HasA protein by an ABC transporter
results.note: Serratia marcescens HasA paper; no claim about Yersinia pseudotuberculosis. Homolog-transfer provenance defect. PMID 8071214.

results.resolved_title: Burkholderia Type VI Secretion Systems Have Distinct Roles in Eukaryotic and Bacterial Cell Interactions
results.quote: Understanding the mechanism of action of T6SS-1, for example by identifying its substrates, will provide insight into the specificity of the secretion apparatus.
results.note: Right organism (B. thailandensis E264) and system (T6SS-1), but the paper characterizes T6SS-1's interbacterial-competition phenotype and never identifies/names TseA-D as secreted effectors; it explicitly defers substrate identification to future work.

results.resolved_title: Burkholderia Type VI Secretion Systems Have Distinct Roles in Eukaryotic and Bacterial Cell Interactions
results.quote: Understanding the mechanism of action of T6SS-1, for example by identifying its substrates, will provide insight into the specificity of the secretion apparatus.
results.note: No TseB effector evidence in text; paper does not name/characterize this protein as a secreted substrate. Substrate identification deferred to future work.

results.resolved_title: Burkholderia Type VI Secretion Systems Have Distinct Roles in Eukaryotic and Bacterial Cell Interactions
results.quote: Understanding the mechanism of action of T6SS-1, for example by identifying its substrates, will provide insight into the specificity of the secretion apparatus.
results.note: No TseC effector evidence in text; paper does not name/characterize this protein as a secreted substrate.

results.resolved_title: Burkholderia Type VI Secretion Systems Have Distinct Roles in Eukaryotic and Bacterial Cell Interactions
results.quote: Understanding the mechanism of action of T6SS-1, for example by identifying its substrates, will provide insight into the specificity of the secretion apparatus.
results.note: No TseD effector evidence in text; paper does not name/characterize this protein as a secreted substrate.

results.resolved_title: G Protein Signaling Events Are Activated at the Leading Edge of Chemotactic Cells
results.note: DOI resolves to a Cell paper (Parent et al.) on G-protein signaling in chemotactic Dictyostelium cells, not the EPEC Tir T3SS effector. Title confirmed via cell.com and sciencedirect (pii S0092867400817845). The genuine Tir paper is Kenny 1997 (DOI 10.1016/s0092-8674(00)80437-7, PMID 9390560).

results.resolved_title: G Protein Signaling Events Are Activated at the Leading Edge of Chemotactic Cells
results.note: Same wrong-DOI defect: resolves to a Dictyostelium chemotaxis/G-protein paper, not the EPEC Tir T3SS paper. Correct source is Kenny 1997, PMID 9390560.

results.resolved_title: The hrpK Operon of Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato DC3000 Encodes Two Proteins Secreted by the Type III (Hrp) Protein Secretion System: HopB1 and HrpK, a Putative Type III Translocator
results.quote: Both HopB1 and HrpK were secreted in culture and translocated into plant cells via the TTSS. ... Taken together, HopB1 is a type III effector
results.note: Quote from abstract; P. syringae pv. tomato DC3000 T3SS. Organism and system match.

results.resolved_title: The hrpK Operon of Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato DC3000 Encodes Two Proteins Secreted by the Type III (Hrp) Protein Secretion System: HopB1 and HrpK, a Putative Type III Translocator
results.quote: Both HopB1 and HrpK were secreted in culture and translocated into plant cells via the TTSS. However, the translocation of HrpK required its C-terminal half.
results.note: HrpK (HrpK1, PSPTO_1380) secreted/translocated by the T3SS of P. syringae DC3000; described as a putative type III translocator. Abstract quote.

results.resolved_title: A Family of T6SS Antibacterial Effectors Related to l,d-Transpeptidases Targets the Peptidoglycan
results.quote: We characterized a Salmonella T6SS antibacterial effector called Tlde1 that is toxic in target-cell periplasm and is neutralized by its cognate immunity protein (Tldi1).
results.note: Abstract (full text paywalled, no PMC; PMID 32579939). Salmonella Typhimurium T6SS effector Tlde1 = STM14_0336. Organism and system match.

results.resolved_title: A Family of T6SS Antibacterial Effectors Related to l,d-Transpeptidases Targets the Peptidoglycan
results.note: Tge2 (STM14_0337) is not named in the retrieved abstract, which describes only Tlde1/Tldi1. Full text paywalled (Elsevier, no PMC), so cannot confirm or refute Tge2.

results.resolved_title: Systematic Identification of Intracellular-Translocated Candidate Effectors in Edwardsiella piscicida
results.quote: two other candidate effectors, ETAE_1303 (named as EseL) and ETAE_2136 (named as EseM) also showed a significantly reduced intracellular transport effectiveness in the T6SS mutant relative to that in the wild type
results.note: ETAE_1303 = EseL, confirmed T6SS-dependent effector. Locus matches batch (ETAE_1303). Edwardsiella (ETAE locus tags).

results.resolved_title: Systematic Identification of Intracellular-Translocated Candidate Effectors in Edwardsiella piscicida
results.quote: two other candidate effectors, ETAE_1303 (named as EseL) and ETAE_2136 (named as EseM) also showed a significantly reduced intracellular transport effectiveness in the T6SS mutant relative to that in the wild type
results.note: ETAE_2136 = EseM, confirmed T6SS-dependent effector. Locus matches batch (ETAE_2136).

results.resolved_title: Host-mediated inflammation disrupts the intestinal microbiota and promotes the overgrowth of Enterobacteriaceae
results.note: DOI (PMID 18030708, Lupp et al.) is about Citrobacter/Campylobacter-induced inflammation and gut microbiota dysbiosis, not the Yersinia YopK/YopQ T3SS effector. No mention of YopK or Yersinia T3SS.

results.resolved_title: Coronatine promotes Pseudomonas syringae virulence in plants by activating a signaling cascade that inhibits salicylic acid accumulation
results.note: DOI (PMID 22704619, Zheng et al.) is about coronatine/NAC transcription factors in Pseudomonas syringae-Arabidopsis, not VopV in Vibrio parahaemolyticus. Wrong protein and wrong organism; no mention of VopV or V. parahaemolyticus T3SS.

results.resolved_title: The Pseudomonas aeruginosa Type VI Secretion PGAP1-like Effector Induces Host Autophagy by Activating Endoplasmic Reticulum Stress
results.quote: we report a detailed investigation of a P. aeruginosa H2-T6SS-dependent phospholipase effector, TplE, which acts as a trans-kingdom toxin.
results.note: Abstract (PMID 27477276; paywalled full text, no PMC). The effector TplE = PA1510 = Tle4 (same locus as batch PA1510). P. aeruginosa H2-T6SS phospholipase effector. Organism and system match.

results.resolved_title: Characterization of the Gene Encoding the Major Secreted Lysophospholipase A of Legionella pneumophila and Its Role in Detoxification of Lysophosphatidylcholine
results.quote: We previously showed that Legionella pneumophila secretes, via its type II secretion system, phospholipase A activities ... we identified and characterized plaA, a gene encoding a phospholipase A that cleaves fatty acids from lysophospholipids.
results.note: Abstract; PlaA (lpg0926) is the major secreted lysophospholipase A of L. pneumophila secreted via T2SS. Organism and system match.

results.resolved_title: BtpB, a novel Brucella TIR-containing effector protein with immune modulatory functions
results.quote: translocation of BtpA-CyaA and CyaA-BtpB was clearly reduced in a virB genetic background, indicating that delivery of both proteins is dependent on the T4SS.
results.note: Full text (PMID 23847770; correct DOI 10.3389/fcimb.2013.00028). BtpB (BAB1_0756) is a Brucella effector translocated by the VirB T4SS. Organism (B. abortus 2308) and system match.

results.resolved_title: CCR5 is a receptor for Staphylococcus aureus leukotoxin ED
results.note: Full text (PMID 23235831) is about Staphylococcus aureus pore-forming leukotoxin LukED and its host receptor CCR5, not the Legionella T4SS effector VipD. Wrong organism (S. aureus, not Legionella), wrong protein, wrong system (pore-forming toxin, not T4SS).

results.resolved_title: LotA, a Legionella deubiquitinase, has dual catalytic activity and contributes to intracellular growth
results.quote: After delivered by the Type 4 secretion system, LotA localises on the LCVs via its PI(3)P-binding domain.
results.note: Abstract (PMID 29543380). LotA (lpg2248) is a Legionella pneumophila Dot/Icm Type 4 secretion system effector. Organism and system match.

results.resolved_title: Dimerization of VirD2 Binding Protein Is Essential for Agrobacterium Induced Tumor Formation in Plants
results.quote: the VirB/D4 system is also involved in the translocation of several effector proteins, including VirE2, VirE3 and VirF into the host cell cytoplasm.
results.note: Full text (PMID 24626239). Agrobacterium VirB/D4 T4SS; VirE3 named as a translocated effector. Paper focus is VBP, but it positively states VirE3 is a T4SS effector. Organism (A. fabrum C58) and system match.

results.resolved_title: The Coxiella burnetii Dot/Icm System Delivers a Unique Repertoire of Type IV Effectors into Host Cells and Is Required for Intracellular Replication
results.note: Full main text retrieved (PMID 21637816); correct organism (C. burnetii) and system (Dot/Icm T4SS). The specific locus CBU1216a does not appear in the retrieved text; the full effector list is in supplementary Tables S1-S3 that were not retrieved. Cannot confirm CBU1216a specifically.

results.resolved_title: Structure of the secretion domain of HxuA from Haemophilus influenzae
results.quote: HxuA is secreted by its specific transporter HxuB. HxuA/HxuB belongs to the so-called two-partner secretion systems (TPSs) that are characterized by a conserved N-terminal domain in the secreted protein which is essential for secretion.
results.note: Abstract (PMID 24316822). HxuA (H. influenzae) secreted via two-partner secretion (TPS = T5b-type/T5SS) by transporter HxuB. Organism and system (T5SS) match.
